nativeRange.habitat: Slow-flowing streams, ponds, marshes, and seasonally flooded wetlands in tropical Asia

summary: A large background stem plant from tropical Asia with fine, feathery light-green foliage that develops a bushy, pine-tree-like silhouette in well-lit aquariums.
careNotes: Giant Ambulia is among the larger stem plants commonly kept in freshwater aquariums — individual stems are commonly reported at 25–50 cm tall and up to 6–15 cm wide, and the plant is best suited as a background centerpiece in mid- to large-sized aquaria (at least 60 cm long, around 100 L or larger). The species is generally considered moderate in difficulty. Medium to medium-high light tends to give the most reliable results, encouraging the horizontal lateral shoots that produce the characteristic bushy, layered form; lower light levels are tolerated but growth becomes sparse and the plant loses its full shape. A nutrient-rich substrate paired with regular water-column fertilisation is generally advised, as growth is fast and the plant is a heavy consumer of macronutrients. Water temperature of 22–28 °C and slightly acidic to neutral conditions (pH roughly 6.0–7.5) are well-tolerated; the species can handle cooler conditions briefly but tends not to thrive below around 20 °C. Giant Ambulia is a distinct species from its smaller relatives — Limnophila sessiliflora (commonly sold as Dwarf Ambulia) and Limnophila aromatica — which have different mature sizes and placement requirements. In open-top aquariums, stems have been reported to grow above the waterline and produce small blue flowers.
breedingNotes: Propagated by trimming the upper portion of mature stems and replanting the cuttings in the substrate; the remaining lower stem readily produces lateral shoots that can also be separated and replanted. Frequent topping is generally advised to encourage bushy lateral growth and prevent leggy stems.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Plant code 046. Primary source for category, growth rate, native origin, light/CO2 demand, and sizing.

additional.name: Flowgrow Aquatic Plant Database
additional.notes: Used as primary secondary because Buce Plant search returned no Limnophila aquatica listing (carries only smaller Limnophila species: aromatica, belem, heterophylla, mini Vietnam). Flowgrow contributed taxonomy (Plantaginaceae), kH range (2–18 dKH), pH (6.5–7.5), CO2 dosing range (10–40 mg/L), and confirmation of Sulawesi/Indonesia in the native range alongside India and Sri Lanka.

additional.name: Fish Laboratory
additional.notes: Cross-checked sizing (23–51 cm), temperature (24–27 °C), pH (6.2–7.1), and propagation method (stem cuttings grouped in clusters). Rates difficulty as easy vs Tropica/Flowgrow's medium; intermediate used as the middle value per playbook averaging rule.

additional.name: Wikipedia
additional.notes: Taxonomy verification — confirmed family Plantaginaceae, order Lamiales, and native range (Asia, Sri Lanka, India).
